unit: U2 — project create + linear.json marker utilities

contract.goal: Ship linear-loom project create <slug> --linear-project=<id>: verifies the Linear Project exists, ensures a workspace-scoped loom-project:<slug> IssueLabel exists (idempotent — lookup first, create if missing), writes the per-slug marker at projects/<slug>/linear.json. Non-destructive: errors with project-already-exists when the marker already exists.
acceptance_criteria: cli/lib/marker.ts: LinearMarker type + read/write/exists/path/labelForSlug utilities + DI'd MarkerIO. Structured errors per failure mode.
cli/verbs/project.ts: projectCreate async verb + PROJECT_VERBS map. Non-destructive on existing marker.
cli/linear-loom.ts: VERB_HANDLERS map; missing-verb / unknown-verb error paths with candidates; configure (verbless) still works.
Unit tests colocated; npm test green.
CLI smokes: project → missing-verb; project bogus → unknown-verb; project create → missing-slug; project create my-thing → missing-linear-project.
rules_applied: DESIGN.md § 4 (per-slug marker is sole source of truth; no defaults).
DESIGN.md § 5 (Linear schema mapping — Project umbrella, IssueLabel for identity).
DESIGN.md § 15 retry policy inherited from U1's LinearClient.
DI pattern continues from U1 (ProjectContext threads client, resolveAuthFn, projectsRoot, markerIO, now).
disqualifiers: Implementing project read or status (U3/U4 territory).

actions: Wrote cli/lib/marker.ts: LinearMarker type (schema_version=1, slug, linear_project_id, linear_project_name, label, created); readMarker/writeMarker/markerExists/markerPath/labelForSlug utilities; DI'd MarkerIO with readFile/writeFile/exists/mkdir hooks; structured errors per failure mode (marker-unreadable, marker-unparseable, marker-malformed, marker-schema-version).

Wrote cli/verbs/project.ts: projectCreate async verb; positional <slug> + --linear-project=<id>; verifies Linear Project via GraphQL; idempotent label flow (lookup by exact name, create if missing); writes marker with trailing newline JSON; tracks label.created boolean in output; exports PROJECT_VERBS map.
Modified cli/linear-loom.ts: introduced VERB_HANDLERS map alongside existing VERBLESS_HANDLERS; added missing-verb and unknown-verb dispatch paths with candidates lists; configure dispatch unchanged.

Wrote 2 colocated *.test.ts: 10 marker tests (path composition, label-for-slug, read happy path + 4 error modes, write with mkdir-first, markerExists boundary delegation) + 8 project tests (missing-slug, missing-linear-project, project-already-exists, linear-project-not-found, label-create-happy, label-already-exists-reuse, label-create-failed, --pretty).

Verified: npm test → 691 passing (was 673; +18). linear-loom project → missing-verb with [create] candidate. linear-loom project bogus → unknown-verb. linear-loom project create → missing-slug. All structured JSON.

Ran panel: derive-panel returned contract-fit + react-api + test-unit + naming. First run returned 3 approved + 1 parse-failure (test-unit hit maxTurns without emitting VERDICT). Re-ran test-unit with tighter packet (do-not-run-anything + one-spot-check); approved. Aggregated: approved.
corrections: Mid-development: initial draft used `!labelResult.id.startsWith('existing')` as a created/reused indicator — a flimsy string-based hack on the label ID. Refactored into an explicit `created: boolean` field carried alongside id/name on the labelResult object.

Panel iteration: test-unit evaluator exhausted its turn budget on first run (parse-failure: no VERDICT line). Retried with a tighter packet emphasizing 'do not run anything, one spot-check, emit VERDICT immediately.' Approved on second run. The first-run packet probably had too many possible threads (npm test + lint + grep + multiple file reads). Lesson for future packets: lead with 'DO NOT run commands' when the goal is verdict-by-spot-check.

changes_since_previous: First unit on the ev-agent.linear-loom.project-create branch (stacked on top of project-verbs / PR #35). Stacked branch posture per the bundled-PR-revisited grill that fired when U1 alone landed at +976 lines. Substrate-shared foundation (LinearClient, auth, errors) lands in #35; this branch adds verb-specific code only.
notes_for_pr: Idempotent label creation: lookup by exact name first; create only if missing. The Linear API doesn't have an upsert mutation for IssueLabel, so the lookup-then-create pattern is the standard idempotency shape. Output payload includes label.created boolean so the operator can tell which path was taken.

Marker file is non-destructive: project create errors with project-already-exists if projects/<slug>/linear.json exists. The operator deletes the marker manually to re-create (or chooses a different slug). This matches loom's manifest.json non-destructive create posture.

Workspace-scoped label (no teamId in the issueLabelCreate input). Per Linear's API contract, omitting teamId creates a workspace-level label — appropriate for a substrate-identity label that needs to span any team hosting a linear-loom project. Sandbox smoke test will confirm the API call shape against the real Linear schema; mocked unit tests verify the request structure only.

Substrate-shared dispatch: linear-loom.ts now distinguishes VERBLESS_HANDLERS (configure) from VERB_HANDLERS (project namespace). The dispatcher reads naturally as: namespace → check verbless → check verb-handlers → fall through to not-implemented. Future verb additions (project read, project status, configure-as-verbless, eventual checkin / task verbs) plug into one map or the other.

test-unit evaluator panel-iteration story: first run exhausted maxTurns without emitting VERDICT (treated as parse-failure per the substrate spec). Retry with a tighter packet ('do not run any commands, one spot-check, emit VERDICT') succeeded. The failure-mode pattern is worth noting for future packets — when a packet implies multiple verification threads, evaluators may try to walk them all and run out of budget.
